Deterministic API Cache Guide

This guide describes a deterministic cache strategy for API clients.

Core concepts

  • ETag-based revalidation with If-None-Match.
  • TTL cache with explicit expiry metadata.
  • Exponential backoff for retry after transient failures.

Warnings and limits

Warning: stale data can appear during upstream outages.

Limitation: retries are capped at 3 attempts per request.

Evidence snippets

"Use ETag and If-None-Match to avoid unnecessary payload downloads."

"Never cache responses that include user-specific secrets."

Not covered

This document does not cover authentication flows, key rotation, or distributed cache invalidation.
